title: EoE production vs episode 25 and 26 vs original
display_title: EoE production vs episode 25 and 26 vs original
wikitext:
[]: EoE production vs episode 25 and 26 vs original             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Mon May 05, 2014 8:04 am Wow, there, before you think I'm trying to say which one is the right version of the story, I'm not. My question in its entirety is: What were episode 25 and 26 originally going to look like if they had the budget? Where I'm coming from: I've only recently (about half a year ago, maybe more) started watching Eva. When I was 13 or 14 I tried watching it, but couldn't get into it. However more than 10 years later I loved it, a lot. It was ambigues, hard to follow, but super interesting. After watching it through (episode 25 and 26 first and then EoE) I decided to watch it again, but with the Evageeks wiki at my side. My mission was to understand every thing that was said and every term that was thrown at me. I think I did pretty well, however things like Kawaru's and Seele's motivations still elude me, sadly. My opinion of End of Evangelion was that it was awesome, totally made me question everything, and it was simply gorgeous (and brutally ugly when it had to be). When I read somewhere that EoE was meant to piss of fans who didn't like the first ending it made it so much better. Why this issue confuses me: There are several things stated. Most importantly is "Episode 25 and 26 are the ending Anno intended and still defends as being better than End of Evangelion". We can safely say that Anno had an aversion to people who didn't like the original ending by showing death threats and slander in EoE. Also something I feel is important to consider is that Episode 25 and 26 were pretty much a happy end and show Shinji overcoming himself and showing the beginnings of him loving himself and becoming a better person. EoE however shows Shinji masturbating and outright trying to kill Asuka when she doesn't want to help him (when, as the first ending shows, he should start with loving himself and not looking for help with others). So 25-26 = good ending and happy Shinji ending. EoE = bad ending and unhappy Shinji ending. However... 25-26 has a few shots (Ritsuko and Misato being dead for one) that are also in EoE so at least a version of the story in EoE was considered. I've read in places that EoE was the planned ending of the series, but that it was rewritten because there wasn't any money left to animate it, so they went for the internal struggle and the lower budget option. Now, Evangelion is of course very much part of Anno in that Anno was depressed and Eva helped him to deal with that depression and end up on top. I'm gonna go out on a limb and say that 25-26 being a positive ending is one of the thing Anno likes about it. So... what's the order of things happening? 1. 25 and 26 are being storyboarded and written with the overall plot of EoE, however with a happy ending to Shinji's character. 2. It turns out there's no more budget to actually do that ending, so a few of the shots that WERE made are used (Misato, Ritsuko being dead, the Misato/Kaji sex scene) are included in a new version that's more about the internal struggle. 3. Fans don't like it, send hate mail. Anno becomes disappointed and perhaps angry at the fans. 4. Evangelion earns a shitload of cash, Anno is asked to make the ending he wanted in the first place, while being happy with the original ending. He makes EoE but makes the ending bad, especially for Shinji. Makes Shinji even more whiny, makes Asuka supercool but then mames and kills her. Makes a naked Rei, but then makes her completely terrifying. In other words, giving the haters what they want, but giving it in such a mangled way they don't like it at all. However, that to me makes no sense for the simple reason that an Episode is about 23 minutes long and each part of EoE is 45 minutes long, almost twice as much screentime. I've seen an original version of what episode 25 and 26 was going to be and it was very VERY close to EoE. It's on the wiki too. It makes no sense that that version of episode 25 and the first part of EoE are THAT close. So what is it? Is anything I said disputable or wrong? What was the original idea for episode 25/26 that was scrapped? Was the original idea a bad ending for Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Mon May 05, 2014 12:12 pm Hoo, this is gonna be a long one but here goes! To answer your topic question, it seems that Episodes 25 at least was going to be how the first half of EoE (Air/Love is Destructive) played out, albeit somewhat condensed due to the shortened runtime: invasion of NERV HQ, battle against harpies, all that jazz! (Keep in mind that EoE's second half does not reflect the original intentions for Episode 26 as far as we know) However, because of the enormous amount of violence and sexual elements presented in it, the network executives shot down the proposed plot and ordered Gainax to make something else. Since they ran out of money and animation cels by this point, Anno decided to basically do a psychological ending that would go into the characters' personalities instead. I imagine it bothered him to change the ending at first, but it seems that he soon admired this new ending as he defended its artistic integrity when criticism came along, of which there was some. But death threats?! Not really, no. There's a page on EvaGeeks where the 'death threats' seen in EoE are translated (http://wiki.evageeks.org/End_of_Evangelion_Death_Threats) and as you can see, they're anything but that. Most of them are adulation for the Death & ReBirth compilation movie released a few months before EoE, not negative reactions to the TV ending. The only letter that could come anywhere close to a death threat is an extremely out of content 'ANNO I'LL KILL YOU!' that could've been about his unkempt goatee for all we know. Yes, people did likely get frustrated about the ending, but I have the feeling Anno would've remade the ending regardless. After all, he's spent the last few years redoing Eva through the Rebuild movies, so we do know that there were a number of flaws in the original show he wanted to fix with a higher budget. It wouldn't be too surprising to find that back in '96, he wanted to do the intended ending now that he had the time and budget to do so. If he really didn't want to do it, he could've left it at that and we'd have missed out on a damn fantastic movie. (One of my all-time favourites, actually) To sum the whole thing up: 1. Original Episode 25 (or first half of EoE) is planned but scrapped due to censorship issues. 2. Brand new, low-budget is made - becomes Episodes 25 & 26 we know of today. 3. Though Anno defends artistic integrity of TV ending from some detractors, he decides to use the larger amounts of time and money to redo Original Episode 25 and make completely new Episode 26 (or second half of EoE) I hope that explains a lot, but I want to tackle one last point. The 'happy ending' part. I don't think the ending to EoE is actually a bad ending; humanity is allowed to come back if they want to, Shinji is determined to stay in reality and find something for himself, the Angels are gone and there's even the chance of Shinji & Asuka reconciling... (why else would she stroke his face while he tried to strangle her?!) It only seems like a bad ending because the presentation (visuals and music) is a lot less upbeat and more sombre compared to the TV ending. - TV ends with Shinji getting congratulated by every other character in the show while 'Good, or Don't Be' is the last song to play in the show - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nWEt_VgLOWY - Movie ends with Shinji collapsing into tears after trying to strangle Asuka on a beach surrounded by a red ocean, a desolate landscape, petrified EVAs and the decaying remains of Giant Naked Rei; with Yume No Sukima being the last song you hear in the film - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FuoOyLLZWKo One ending is not necessarily happier than the other when you get down to it; EoE's depressing presentation when Shinji returns to reality simply puts his resolve to the test. It's all good to say 'I can exist in this world!' but it's for naught if you can't put that philosophy into action. Anyway, that's my interpretation on the whole matter. I hope it helped you figure things out and until we meet again, have a great day! :)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 05, 2014 12:20 pm Wasn't there some interview where Anno says he went, "Guys, pleeeease let me do this weird Art thing, I promise we'll do another version later?" In any case, what they ran out of was time, not cash.

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Mon May 05, 2014 1:04 pm FrDougal9000 wrote: A little long for quoting :p

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 05, 2014 1:58 pm Some related threads http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/9158/Yamaga-EoTV-planned-from-the-start-EoE-was-an-afterthought/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10029/Okada-Evas-Ending-Was-Decided-at-the-Last-Moment/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15439/Which-ending-is-the-TRUE-ending-of-the-original-EVA/

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 05, 2014 6:04 pm Kendrix has the right of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Mon May 05, 2014 6:40 pm Anno had a Q&A late last year where he was asked about the end of the series and about Asuka being moe and answered that 25' (AKA Air/Love is destructive) was the originally planned episode for the TV series episode 25, but they ran out of time. EOTV26 was the original intended ending all along, but when Anno decided to do EOE, he decided to redo ep. 26 as well to make it "more beautiful."

[]:             Quote Post by Dougurasu » Tue May 06, 2014 1:49 am Yeah while going through Mr Tines links I found that Q&A :p So, wow, that's a bit more complicated than I thought the answer would be :p thanks everyone :p
